chunks.text: Course: EE C220B | EECS at UC Berkeley EE C220B. Experiential Advanced Control Design I Catalog Description: Experience-based learning in the design of SISO and MIMO feedback controllers for linear systems. The student will master skills needed to apply linear control design and analysis tools to classical and modern control problems. In particular, the participant will be exposed to and develop expertise in two key control design technologies: frequency-domain control synthesis and time-domain optimization-based approach. Units: 3 Also Offered As: MECENG C231A, ELENG C220B Formats: Spring: 3 hours of lecture and 2 hours of laboratory per week Fall: 3 hours of lecture and 2 hours of laboratory per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: Yes Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
